home.title: AI agent setup — in seconds
home.lead: AI Kit generates configuration for Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, Aider, and Qwen Code from a single manifest. One prompt, one binary — and your repo is ready for AI-assisted development across every runner you use.

what.title: Everything an AI agent needs to start working in your repo
what.sub: AI Kit consumes a single .aikit/manifest.yaml and produces per-runner artifacts: CLAUDE.md and .claude/agents/*.md for Claude Code, .cursor/rules/*.mdc for Cursor, AGENTS.md and opencode.json for OpenCode, .aider.conf.yml and CONVENTIONS.md for Aider, .qwen/settings.json for Qwen Code. Same source of truth, five output shapes.

providers.title: Five runners
providers.body: Generate configs for Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, Aider, and Qwen Code — pick any subset via render_targets in the manifest.

languages.title: Any stack
languages.body: Declare your stack and modules in the manifest; per-agent prompts and rules adapt to it via shared snippets.

planning.title: Verify before write
planning.body: Run verify to validate the manifest end-to-end (schema, references, model resolution, file collisions) before any file lands on disk.

agents.title: Agent definitions
agents.body: Per-agent prompt bodies are wrapped by family-specific dialects (Anthropic XML, OpenAI markdown, Qwen DO/DON'T) at render time.

quickstart.title: Quick Start
quickstart.body: The recommended way — paste a single prompt into Claude Code or OpenCode and let the agent draft the manifest and run the binary.

cli.title: CLI Usage
cli.body: Prefer running things yourself? Download the binary and call verify and generate against your manifest.

files.title: Generated Files
files.body: See exactly what AI Kit produces per render target.

build.title: Build from Source
build.body: Compile the binary yourself with Gradle and Kotlin/Native.

quickstart.title: From zero to a configured project
quickstart.lead: The fastest way: paste a single prompt into Claude Code or OpenCode inside your project.
quickstart.step1: Open Claude Code or OpenCode in your project directory.
quickstart.step2: Paste the prompt below and let the agent take it from there.
quickstart.step3: It inspects your project, drafts .aikit/manifest.yaml (stack, modules, agents, render targets, providers), runs kit-setup verify (looping on errors), then runs kit-setup generate. You get the rendered file list to confirm.
quickstart.tip: Re-running generate overwrites the kit unconditionally. Edit the manifest and re-run whenever you need to tweak.

cli.title: CLI Usage
cli.lead: Prefer running the binary yourself? Download it from the Releases page and run verify and generate against your manifest.

subcommandsList.version: Prints the binary version and exits.

exitMeanings.one: Manifest invalid (verify) or refused by validation (generate).
exitMeanings.two: Usage error, manifest load failure, parse error, or runtime I/O failure.

files.title: Generated Files
files.lead: What lands in your project per render target. Pick targets via render_targets in the manifest.

purposes.claudemd: Top-level guidance file Claude Code reads on every session — composed from knowledge.constitution sections.
purposes.claudeAgents: Per-agent definitions with frontmatter (name, description, tools, resolved model).
purposes.claudeSkills: Anthropic-style skill bundles wrapped in family-specific XML.
purposes.claudeSettings: Permissions, MCP servers, and env vars for Claude Code.
purposes.cursorRules: Per-section .mdc rule files (constitution split across files, alwaysApply: true).
purposes.cursorPrompts: User prompt templates as scoped Cursor rules.
purposes.opencodeSettings: OpenCode project configuration with provider + MCP block.
purposes.agentsMd: Top-level guidance file shared by OpenCode and Qwen Code (don't enable both at once — they collide).
purposes.aiderConf: Global Aider configuration with model binding.
purposes.qwenSettings: Qwen Code settings, including modelProviders block.

build.title: Build from Source
build.lead: If you want to compile the binary yourself, you'll need JDK 21.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.lead: Quick answers to common questions about AI Kit.

items.q: Does the kit overwrite existing files?
items.a: Yes. Every generate run overwrites unconditionally. If you've customised CLAUDE.md or any other generated file, edit the manifest or its referenced templates rather than the output — the next generate will clobber direct edits.

items.q: Which render targets should I pick?
items.a: The ones you actually use. Picking too many is fine, but opencode + qwen-code together collide on AGENTS.md and verify will reject the combo — drop one.

items.q: What if my language isn't in the list?
items.a: Declare it under stack.languages. The manifest is free-form there; the values surface into prompts via shared snippets.
Open an issue on the GitHub repository — link in the footer.
